questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The focal length of a convex lens is positive.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because the focal length is the distance between the pole and the principal focus.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The focal length of a convex lens is indeed positive, and this is because it is measured from the pole to the principal focus, where the rays converge.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A concave mirror can form a real and inverted image.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because the mirror can converge rays to a point, forming a real image.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: A concave mirror can indeed form a real and inverted image, and this is because of its ability to converge rays to a point.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The angular magnification of a compound microscope is given by M = -di/do.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is the formula for angular magnification, where di is the image distance and do is the object distance.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The angular magnification of a compound microscope is indeed given by the formula M = -di/do, and this is because it represents the ratio of the angle subtended by the image to the angle subtended by the object.
